Reserved public IP created and assigned: 158.180.52.169; Terraform plan now no-change; Terraform state backed up to gopass dev/oci/oracle-cloud-nixos/terraform-state and terraform-state.backup. Oracle deploy updated static relay endpoint and deploy defaults to 158.180.52.169. title: Doom module extraction: tools/agent-shell + os/ewm
description:
Extract all agent-shell/LLM config from ~/.config/doom/config.el into a proper Doom module at ~/.config/doom/modules/tools/agent-shell/ (config.el + packages.el). Also create ~/.config/doom/modules/os/ewm/ (config.el + packages.el) for the EWM Wayland compositor.

Current state:
- config.el has ~200 lines of agent-shell config inline (Pi primary, Claude secondary, 10 ecosystem packages)
- packages.el has all agent packages inline
- Agent-recall-org.el in lisp/ should move into the agent-shell module (PR #5 merging native support will obsolete it — drop it, pin agent-recall to PR branch)
- EWM module is empty shell for now (keybindings come in home-manager-nap)

Key decisions already made:
- Module name: tools/agent-shell (not tools/llm — we only use agent-shell)
- Pi is primary agent, Claude is secondary (agent-shell-pi-make-agent-config)
- Sidebar defaults to Pi
- init.el needs (agent-shell) and (ewm) flags
- meta-agent-shell config goes in the module too

Files to modify: ~/.config/doom/config.el, ~/.config/doom/packages.el, ~/.config/doom/init.el
Files to create: ~/.config/doom/modules/tools/agent-shell/{config.el,packages.el}, ~/.config/doom/modules/os/ewm/{config.el,packages.el}
Files to delete: ~/.config/doom/lisp/agent-recall-org.el (after agent-recall PR #5 merges)
close_reason: Extracted Doom agent-shell and ewm modules; doom sync passed.

title: Update AGENTS.md: document pi-agenticoding/pi-kb/pi-paster overlap guidance
description:
Add overlap guidance to AGENTS.md for the three new Pi packages:

1. pi-subagents vs pi-agenticoding spawn:
   - pi-subagents: orchestrator-led fanout/chains (subagent() tool, chain definitions)
   - pi-agenticoding spawn: LLM-invoked isolated child agents inside a session
   - Rule: use pi-subagents for structured orchestration, spawn for ad-hoc isolation

2. context-mode vs pi-kb vs pi-agenticoding notebook:
   - context-mode: session FTS5 sandbox (indexed content, searchable, ephemeral per project)
   - pi-kb: curated durable wiki (LLM-compiled markdown, persists at ~/.pi/agent/kb/)
   - pi-agenticoding notebook: agent-maintained sparse working notes (session-scoped pages)
   - Rule: context-mode for session search, pi-kb for permanent reference, notebook for working state

3. Compaction: pi-agenticoding handoff replaces built-in compaction (compaction.enabled=false).
   Agent uses handoff tool at ~80% context. Watchdog warns at 30/50/70%.

Add to the agent orchestration section of ~/.pi/agent/AGENTS.md.
close_reason: Added Pi package overlap guidance to ~/.pi/agent/AGENTS.md and validated inserted diff.

title: EWM compositor setup: cargo install, GDM .desktop, Doom keybindings
description:
Set up EWM (Emacs Wayland Manager) as daily-driver compositor on Guix System.

EWM repo: https://codeberg.org/ezemtsov/ewm
Old EXWM config: ~/Downloads/exwm-config-bak.el and ~/.exwm (identical, 150 lines)

Step 1: cargo install from EWM repo (test on hardware first)
Step 2: Create GDM .desktop session file (replaces emacs-exwm + emacs-desktop-environment in system.scm)
Step 3: Minimal Doom keybindings in modules/os/ewm/config.el:
  - s-hjkl windmove, s-1..5 workspaces, s-d app launch, doom leader
  - Skip: media keys, systray daemons, redshift (add incrementally)
Step 4: Update guix/system.scm: replace emacs-exwm packages with EWM binary path

Current Guix session setup: GDM (wayland? #t) → selectable desktop session
Current system packages: (list "nix" "emacs-exwm" "emacs" "xdg-dbus-proxy" "emacs-desktop-environment")
set-xorg-configuration block has keyboard/touchpad config

Key decision: Option 3 (cargo install to test) then proper Guix package later.
EWM is Wayland-native, Rust dynamic module, must run from TTY (DRM backend).
close_reason: EWM flake input + overlay + Guix session .desktop + Doom config.el + ewm-session wrapper. Deploy with just deploy-mahakala-system + just deploy-mahakala-hm + doom sync

title: Enable Oracle NixOS auto-upgrade maintenance
description: Configure the Oracle NixOS host for low-touch maintenance: periodic system auto-upgrade/update from this flake, safe scheduling, and automatic Nix store cleanup/optimization where appropriate. Preserve unrelated dirty work. Validate eval and deploy if safe.
close_reason: Completed Oracle auto-upgrade and cleanup maintenance; deployed and verified timers

comments.author: worldofgeese
comments.text: Started. Scope: Oracle host auto-upgrade/autoupdate/easy maintenance only; preserve unrelated dirty files.

comments.author: worldofgeese
comments.text: Added system.autoUpgrade (github:worldofgeese/den#oracle), nix.gc, nix.optimise to modules/oracle/_configuration.nix; documented in docs/oracle/reference-operations.md. Eval + just check pass.

comments.author: worldofgeese
comments.text:
Paphos precedent review (worker):

**paphos cleanup via den.aspects.server** (modules/server.nix, included in defaults.nix):
- nix.gc.automatic = true
- nix.gc.options = "--delete-older-than 8d"
- no nix.optimise in repo server convention

**paphos autoUpgrade** (modules/paphos/system.nix):
- flake = /etc/nixos#paphos (local checkout)
- Wed 03:00 weekly, 30min jitter, allowReboot = false
- flags: --update-input nixpkgs + forgesync

**Oracle chosen settings** (modules/oracle/_configuration.nix):
- nix.gc matches server.nix exactly (8d retention)
- nix.optimise.automatic = true — kept beyond paphos baseline; daily GitHub auto-upgrade on headless OCI VM benefits store compaction
- system.autoUpgrade: github:worldofgeese/den#oracle, daily 04:00 + 45min jitter, allowReboot true with 04:00–05:00 window (cloud-appropriate; paphos allowReboot=false is for always-on build host)

No config changes needed after comparison — existing diff already aligns gc with repo convention while preserving Oracle-specific auto-upgrade/reboot posture.

Validation: alejandra --check pass; nix eval toplevel.drvPath OK; maintenance options eval confirms enable/autoUpgradeFlake/gc/optimise values.

comments.author: worldofgeese
comments.text: Fixed autoUpgrade flags: removed --update-input nixpkgs (remote GitHub flake cannot mutate lockfile on host). Kept --print-build-logs. Updated docs/oracle/reference-operations.md to explain oracle tracks GitHub main flake.lock; input bumps happen via repo push (just update), unlike paphos local /etc/nixos checkout. Validation: nix eval toplevel OK; autoUpgrade.flags eval shows no update-input; alejandra --check OK.

comments.author: worldofgeese
comments.text: Deployed Oracle auto-maintenance. Config now enables system.autoUpgrade from github:worldofgeese/den#oracle with --refresh/--print-build-logs and no --update-input (remote flake lock controlled by pushed main), allows reboot only in 04:00-05:00 window, enables nix.gc automatic --delete-older-than 8d, and nix.optimise automatic. Live verification: nixos-upgrade.timer, nix-gc.timer, nix-optimise.timer enabled/listed; upgrade script contains github flake and no update-input; Tailscale relay remained active.

title: Investigate child-agent behavioral_directive injected into orchestrator context
description: Orchestrator session has <behavioral_directive> saying 'You are a focused child agent spawned by a parent agent' — causes spawn failures and role confusion. Likely source: pi-subagents config, pi.nix settings, or compaction/handoff injecting stale directive. Check: pi-subagents package hooks, settings.json subagents config, session start hooks, and whether handoff/compaction summary carries the directive forward.
close_reason: Root cause: upstream pi-agenticoding bug — handoff/compact doesn't strip behavioral_directive tags from spawn prompt history. Not fixable in home-manager repo. Documented in bead comments.

comments.author: worldofgeese
comments.text: Root cause identified: pi-agenticoding handoff/compaction bug. spawn/index.ts:229 injects 'You are a focused child agent...' into child task prompts. When parent session gets compacted, those spawn prompts in session history survive as <roles> entries in session_resume. One then gets promoted to a top-level <behavioral_directive> tag by the session system. Fix: handoff/compact.ts should strip behavioral_directive from compacted output, OR session system should verify actual child status before honoring the tag. This is upstream in pi-agenticoding npm package — not fixable in home-manager repo.
